summary: date-format-helper2 postinstall exfiltrates Coze tokens, /root/.npmrc, and workspace.env/credentials to attacker OOB host
details: The package advertises itself as a React date-formatting utility, but postinstall.js executes a credential-harvesting script on `npm install`. It enumerates every process.env key containing 'COZE'/'coze' and POSTs them to http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com (a requestrepo.com out-of-band capture host) over plain HTTP. It then uses the harvested COZE_WORKLOAD_API_TOKEN to call api.coze.cn/api.coze.com and ships those API responses to the same attacker host. In parallel, postinstall.js shells out to `find /workspace -name ".env" -o...` to locate.env /.env.* / config.json / credentials.json under /workspace and POSTs their contents, and runs `cat /root/.npmrc` to capture npm registry auth tokens and POST them to the same endpoint. The benign date-formatting code in index.js is cover; the package's only real effect on installation is targeted credential theft from environments running Coze workloads (CI/sandbox).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
